actual_output:
I can do that. I need your search criteria first so I don’t surface roles that are a poor fit.

### Job Search Setup — Required Criteria

Please provide:

| Criteria | Your Preference |
|---|---|
| Target role titles | e.g., Product Manager, Data Analyst, Backend Engineer |
| Location | City/state, remote only, hybrid, relocation open? |
| Minimum salary | Base or total comp minimum |
| Experience level | Entry, mid, senior, lead, executive |
| Work authorization | Country + whether sponsorship is needed |
| Role type | Full-time, contract, part-time, internship |
| Industries to prioritize | e.g., fintech, healthcare, AI, climate, SaaS |
| Industries to avoid | Optional |
| Must-have skills | Tools, languages, certifications, domain expertise |
| Nice-to-have skills | Optional |
| Dealbreakers | e.g., onsite 5 days/week, travel, commission-heavy |

### Optional but Helpful

If you want stronger matching, send one of these:

1. Your current resume  
2. LinkedIn profile text  
3. A short background summary like:

> “I’m a mid-level data analyst with 4 years in SQL, Tableau, Python, and healthcare analytics. Looking for remote U.S. roles, $100K+, no sponsorship needed.”

Once I have that, I’ll return a prioritized job shortlist with match scores, salary/location fit, application difficulty, and recommended resume keywords for each role.
judge_reasoning: The chatbot correctly identified that it lacked the necessary parameters to fulfill the request and provided a structured way for the user to provide that information.
